title: Add chaos E2E tests to CI pipeline
description:
# GitHub Issue #165: Add chaos E2E tests to CI pipeline

## Summary

The chaos test framework introduced in CC-0047 (12a8c92) provides Chainsaw-based chaos E2E tests using Chaos Mesh `PodChaos` CRDs (MariaDB, Memcached, OpenBao pod-kill scenarios). These tests are currently only runnable locally via `make e2e-chaos` but are **not integrated into the CI pipeline**.

As more chaos test scenarios are planned, CI integration is needed to ensure operator resilience is continuously validated.

## Current state

- Three chaos scenarios exist under `tests/e2e-chaos/`:
  - `mariadb-pod-kill` (SC-CHAOS-001)
  - `memcached-pod-kill` (SC-CHAOS-002)
  - `openbao-pod-kill` (SC-CHAOS-003)
- A dedicated Chainsaw config exists at `tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml` with serial execution and extended timeouts (5m assert, 2m cleanup)
- A `make e2e-chaos` target exists in the Makefile
- No reference to chaos tests exists in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`

## Requirements

- [ ] Add a `e2e-chaos` job to the CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yaml`)
- [ ] The job needs a kind cluster with Chaos Mesh installed (in addition to the standard E2E infrastructure)
- [ ] Wire up change detection so chaos tests run when `tests/e2e-chaos/**` or operator/shared code changes
- [ ] Chaos tests must run **after** the regular E2E tests pass (they depend on a working baseline)
- [ ] Collect JUnit reports and diagnostics artifacts on failure (as done for other E2E jobs)
- [ ] Consider whether chaos tests should be required to pass for merge or run as informational/non-blocking initially

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/165

stories.title: CI pipeline runs chaos E2E tests on relevant code changes
stories.want: chaos E2E tests to run automatically in the CI pipeline when operator code or chaos test definitions change
stories.so_that: operator resilience is continuously validated without manual intervention
criteria: A `e2e-chaos` job exists in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
The job triggers when `tests/e2e-chaos/**` files change
The job triggers when Go operator or shared code changes
The job triggers on `v*` tag pushes
The job does NOT trigger on docs-only or helm-only changes

stories.title: Chaos tests run after regular E2E tests verify baseline
stories.want: chaos tests to run only after the regular E2E operator tests pass or are skipped
stories.so_that: chaos tests do not waste CI resources testing a broken operator baseline
criteria: The `e2e-chaos` job depends on `e2e-operator` completing without failure
The `e2e-chaos` job depends on pre-flight checks (lint, test, test-integration, shellcheck, verify-codegen) completing without failure
The `e2e-chaos` job still runs when `e2e-operator` is skipped (chaos-only changes)
The `e2e-chaos` job is blocked when any pre-flight check fails

stories.title: Chaos test failures are diagnosable via CI artifacts
stories.want: JUnit reports and diagnostic information to be collected when chaos tests run
stories.so_that: I can diagnose failures without reproducing them locally
criteria: JUnit report is uploaded as artifact named `e2e-chaos-junit-report` with 14-day retention
Diagnostic information (pod status, logs, events, Chaos Mesh status) is dumped on failure via `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh`
The JUnit report name is distinct from other E2E report artifacts

stories.title: Chaos test failures do not block PR merges initially
stories.want: chaos tests to run as non-blocking (informational) initially
stories.so_that: newly integrated chaos tests do not block development while their stability is being proven in CI
criteria: The `e2e-chaos` job uses `continue-on-error: true`
Publish jobs (`build-and-push`, `helm-push`) do NOT depend on `e2e-chaos`
The overall workflow status is not affected by chaos test failures
A YAML comment documents the non-blocking rationale and when to revisit

stories.title: CI documentation reflects chaos test integration
stories.want: the CI workflow and chaos test reference docs to accurately describe the chaos CI job
stories.so_that: I can understand when and how chaos tests run in CI without reading the workflow YAML
criteria: `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` includes the `e2e-chaos` job in the Job Dependency DAG
`docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` documents the `e2e-chaos` job configuration
`docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` CI Trigger Policy section is updated from placeholder to actual implementation
Change detection filters for `e2e_chaos` are documented

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL include an `e2e-chaos` job that runs all Chainsaw chaos E2E tests
requirements.rationale: Continuous validation of operator resilience prevents regressions in fault recovery behavior

scenarios.name: Chaos job exists and runs chaos tests
scenarios.when: the CI pipeline runs with `e2e-chaos` change detection output set to `true`
scenarios.then: a job named `e2e-chaos` executes `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e-chaos/`
and_then: all 3 chaos scenarios (mariadb-pod-kill, memcached-pod-kill, openbao-pod-kill) are executed serially

scenarios.name: Chaos job deploys full infrastructure and operator
scenarios.then: the job creates a kind cluster, builds and loads the keystone operator and service images, deploys the infrastructure stack (including Chaos Mesh), and deploys the keystone operator
and_then: the operator webhook is ready before chaos tests begin

scenarios.name: Chaos job uses dedicated Chainsaw config
scenarios.then: Chainsaw uses `tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml` with 5m assert timeout, 2m cleanup timeout, and serial execution
and_then: the happy-path config at `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml` is NOT used

requirements.description: The change detection SHALL trigger chaos tests when `tests/e2e-chaos/**` files or Go operator/shared code changes
requirements.rationale: Chaos tests validate operator resilience, so they must run when operator code changes or when chaos test definitions change

scenarios.name: Chaos test file changes trigger job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies only files under `tests/e2e-chaos/`
scenarios.then: the `changes` job outputs `e2e-chaos=true`
and_then: the `e2e-chaos` job is triggered

scenarios.name: Go shared code changes trigger job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies files under `internal/` or `go.work`
scenarios.then: the `changes` job outputs `e2e-chaos=true` (via `go_changed=true`)
and_then: both `e2e-operator` and `e2e-chaos` are triggered

scenarios.name: Operator code changes trigger job
scenarios.then: the `changes` job outputs `e2e-chaos=true` (via `go_changed=true`)
and_then: both `e2e-operator` and `e2e-chaos` are triggered

scenarios.name: Tag pushes trigger job
scenarios.then: all areas are forced active including `e2e-chaos=true`
and_then: the full release pipeline runs including chaos tests

scenarios.name: Docs-only changes do not trigger job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies only files under `docs/`
scenarios.then: the `changes` job outputs `e2e-chaos=false`
and_then: the `e2e-chaos` job is skipped

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL depend on pre-flight checks and the `e2e-operator` job, blocking when either fails
requirements.rationale: Chaos tests assume a working operator baseline; running them against broken code wastes CI resources and produces misleading failures

scenarios.name: Job runs after e2e-operator succeeds
scenarios.when: all pre-flight checks pass and `e2e-operator` succeeds
scenarios.then: the `e2e-chaos` job runs

scenarios.name: Job runs when e2e-operator is skipped
scenarios.when: only chaos test definitions changed (no Go code changes) and `e2e-operator` is skipped
scenarios.then: the `e2e-chaos` job still runs (skipped ≠ failure)
and_then: pre-flight checks are also skipped since no Go code changed, which is acceptable

scenarios.name: Job blocked when pre-flight fails
scenarios.then: the `e2e-chaos` job does not run
and_then: the condition `!contains(needs.*.result, 'failure')` evaluates to false

scenarios.name: Job blocked when e2e-operator fails
scenarios.then: the `e2e-chaos` job does not run
and_then: CI resources are not wasted on chaos tests against a broken operator

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL upload JUnit test reports as workflow artifacts
requirements.rationale: JUnit reports enable CI result visibility in GitHub PR checks and external dashboards

scenarios.name: JUnit report uploaded on success
scenarios.then: the JUnit report at `_output/reports/` is uploaded as artifact `e2e-chaos-junit-report`
and_then: artifact retention is 14 days

scenarios.name: JUnit report uploaded on failure
scenarios.then: the JUnit report is still uploaded (via `if: always()`)
and_then: partial results from tests that ran before failure are included

scenarios.name: Report name is distinct
scenarios.when: multiple E2E jobs run in the same workflow
scenarios.then: the chaos report artifact name `e2e-chaos-junit-report` does not collide with `e2e-infra-junit-report` or `e2e-{operator}-junit-report`

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL dump diagnostic information on failure using the shared diagnostic script
requirements.rationale: Diagnostic output enables root cause analysis without local reproduction, following the established pattern from e2e-infra and e2e-operator jobs

scenarios.name: Diagnostics dumped on failure
scenarios.when: the `e2e-chaos` job fails at any step
scenarios.then: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` runs with `OPERATOR=keystone` to collect HelmRelease status, pod status, DaemonSet status, chaos-daemon details, events, operator logs, CR conditions
and_then: diagnostic output is captured in the job log

scenarios.name: Diagnostics run always
scenarios.then: the diagnostics step executes with `if: always()`
and_then: diagnostics are available even on timeout failures

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL use `continue-on-error: true` for non-blocking behavior initially
requirements.rationale: Chaos tests are newly integrated into CI and may exhibit environmental flakiness (pod restart timing, Chaos Mesh finalization); non-blocking prevents blocking development while stability is proven

scenarios.name: Job failure does not block workflow
scenarios.then: the overall workflow status is not affected (continues as if the job succeeded)
and_then: the job shows a yellow warning icon in the GitHub UI, not a red failure

scenarios.name: Publish jobs unaffected
scenarios.when: the `e2e-chaos` job fails and the workflow is a push to main
scenarios.then: the `build-and-push`, `helm-push`, and `merge-operator-images` jobs proceed normally
and_then: publish jobs do NOT list `e2e-chaos` in their `needs`

scenarios.name: Release pipeline unaffected
scenarios.when: the `e2e-chaos` job fails on a `v*` tag push
scenarios.then: the `github-release` job proceeds normally
and_then: the release is not blocked by chaos test failures

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL deploy a kind cluster with the full infrastructure stack including Chaos Mesh
requirements.rationale: Chaos tests require Chaos Mesh CRDs and controllers to inject PodChaos faults, and the operator needs the full infrastructure stack (MariaDB, Memcached, OpenBao, ESO) as dependencies

scenarios.name: Kind cluster created with standard config
scenarios.then: a kind cluster named `forge-e2e` is created using `hack/kind-config.yaml`
and_then: the cluster has a single control-plane node

scenarios.name: Infrastructure includes Chaos Mesh
scenarios.then: the Chaos Mesh HelmRelease is Ready in the `chaos-mesh` namespace
and_then: PodChaos CRDs are available and the `chaos-daemon` DaemonSet is running

scenarios.name: Keystone operator is deployed and ready
scenarios.then: the keystone operator is deployed via `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` with the locally-built dev image
and_then: the operator webhook is accepting traffic before chaos tests begin

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job SHALL have a timeout of 60 minutes to accommodate serial chaos test execution
requirements.rationale: Chaos tests run serially (parallel: 1) with 5-minute assert timeouts per step. 3 scenarios × 6 steps × up to 5 min each plus 10-15 min cluster setup requires at least 45 min, with 60 min as a safe buffer

scenarios.name: Job timeout set appropriately
scenarios.then: `timeout-minutes: 60` is set on the job
and_then: this exceeds the `e2e-operator` timeout of 45 min to account for serial execution and longer assert windows

scenarios.name: Job does not time out under normal conditions
scenarios.then: the total execution time is well within the 60-minute timeout
and_then: typical expected runtime is 30-45 min (10-15 min setup + 20-30 min tests)

tasks.title: Add `e2e_chaos` path filter and `e2e-chaos` output to ci.yaml changes job (REQ-002, CC-0054)
tasks.description: In `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`, add an `e2e_chaos` filter to the `dorny/paths-filter` step (after the existing `e2e_infra` filter) matching `tests/e2e-chaos/**`. Add `e2e-chaos: ${{ steps.result.outputs.e2e-chaos }}` to the `changes` job outputs. Add `FILTER_e2e_chaos: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.e2e_chaos }}` to the `Resolve effective changes` step env block. Pattern: follow the existing `e2e_infra` filter structure.

tasks.title: Wire `e2e-chaos` output through `hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh` (REQ-002, CC-0054)
tasks.description: In `hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh`, add logic to emit `e2e-chaos` output after the existing operator detection block (after `echo "go=${go_changed}"`). The output should be `true` when `FILTER_e2e_chaos=true` OR `go_changed=true` (covers operator/shared code changes and tag pushes). Add a comment explaining the trigger logic. Verify the script passes `shellcheck --severity=warning`.

tasks.title: Add `e2e-chaos` job to ci.yaml (REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008, CC-0054)
tasks.description: Add the `e2e-chaos` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` between `e2e-operator` and `tempest`. Structure: `needs: [changes, lint, shellcheck, test, test-integration, verify-codegen, e2e-operator]`, `if: needs.changes.outputs.e2e-chaos == 'true' && !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') && !contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled')`, `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`, `timeout-minutes: 60`, `continue-on-error: true`. Steps: (1) checkout, (2) setup-go, (3) create kind cluster (helm/kind-action with hack/kind-config.yaml, cluster forge-e2e), (4) build operator image (`make docker-build OPERATOR=keystone IMG=...`), (5) build service image (hack/ci-build-service-image.sh with OPERATOR=keystone), (6) load images into kind, (7) setup E2E infra (composite action), (8) deploy operator (hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh with OPERATOR=keystone), (9) run chaos tests (`mkdir -p _output/reports && chainsaw test --config tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e-chaos/`), (10) dump diagnostics (`if: always()`, OPERATOR=keystone), (11) upload JUnit report (`if: always()`, name `e2e-chaos-junit-report`, path `_output/reports/`, retention 14 days). Follow the e2e-operator job structure exactly for steps 1-8, simplifying where chaos tests don't need the 2026.1 image or upgrade tag.

tasks.title: Update `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` with e2e-chaos job documentation (REQ-001, CC-0054)
tasks.description: Update `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md`: (1) Add CC-0054 to the frontmatter `feature` field. (2) Update the Job Dependency DAG ASCII diagram to include `e2e-chaos` in the E2E Jobs section with its dependency chain. (3) Add an `### e2e-chaos` subsection (after `e2e-operator`) documenting: trigger condition, dependencies, timeout, continue-on-error rationale, steps table, and change detection filter. (4) Update the job count in the DAG description. Follow the formatting pattern of the existing `### e2e-operator` subsection.

tasks.title: Update `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` CI Trigger Policy section (REQ-001, REQ-002, CC-0054)
tasks.description: Update `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`: (1) Add CC-0054 to the frontmatter `feature` field. (2) Replace the placeholder `CI Trigger Policy (Planned)` section (lines 86-96) with the actual implementation details: change detection triggers (chaos test files, Go code, tag pushes), dependency on e2e-operator, non-blocking behavior (continue-on-error), job timeout, and artifact collection. Remove the `> Note: CI integration is planned for a follow-up PR` callout.

test_specifications.expected: The upload-artifact step uses `name: e2e-chaos-junit-report`, `path: _output/reports/`, `retention-days: 14`, and `if: always()`

test_specifications.story: Chaos test failures are diagnosable via CI artifacts
test_specifications.expected: The diagnostics step runs `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` with `env: OPERATOR: keystone` and `if: always()`

test_specifications.expected: The diagnostics step has `if: always()` so it runs even on timeout or test failures

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline runs chaos E2E tests on relevant code changes

review_criteria: The `e2e-chaos` job YAML is syntactically valid and follows the structure of the existing `e2e-operator` job (same action versions, SHA pins, env patterns)
Change detection correctly outputs `e2e-chaos=true` for chaos test file changes, Go code changes, and tag pushes; `false` for docs/helm-only changes
The `e2e-chaos` job uses `continue-on-error: true` and is NOT listed in the `needs` of any publish or release job
JUnit report artifact name `e2e-chaos-junit-report` does not conflict with existing artifact names (`e2e-infra-junit-report`, `e2e-{operator}-junit-report`)
All YAML comments include the CC-0054 feature ID for traceability
Documentation in `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` and `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` accurately reflects the implemented behavior
The Job Dependency DAG in ci-workflow.md correctly shows `e2e-chaos` dependencies and non-blocking nature
implementation_notes:
## Architectural Decisions

1. **Independent cluster per job**: The `e2e-chaos` job creates its own kind cluster rather than sharing with `e2e-operator`. This follows the existing pattern where each E2E job is isolated. Trade-off: longer total CI time but simpler and more reliable.

2. **Non-blocking via `continue-on-error: true`**: Job-level (not step-level) continue-on-error. This means the entire chaos job is informational. The GitHub UI shows a yellow warning icon for failures but the workflow succeeds. Revisit when chaos test stability is proven over 2-4 weeks of CI runs.

3. **Change detection via `go_changed` flag**: Rather than duplicating operator path filters, the chaos job leverages the existing `go_changed` boolean which is true when ANY Go code (shared or per-operator) changes. This means chaos tests run whenever regular E2E tests would run, plus when chaos test definitions change independently.

4. **Keystone-only (no matrix)**: All 3 chaos scenarios test keystone operator resilience. A matrix strategy is unnecessary. When chaos tests are added for other operators, the job can be converted to a matrix.

5. **No 2026.1 or upgrade images**: Chaos tests use `image.tag: 2025.2` (default release). The 2026.1 build and upgrade tag steps from e2e-operator are omitted to save ~2 min CI time.

## Job Dependency Graph

```
changes ──┬── lint ────────────┐
          ├── shellcheck ──────┤
          ├── test ────────────┤
          ├── test-integration ┤
          ├── verify-codegen ──┤
          │                    ├──> e2e-operator ──┬──> build-and-push ──> merge-images
          │                    │                   ├──> helm-push
          │                    │                   │
          │                    └──> e2e-chaos ─────┘ (non-blocking, no downstream deps)
          ├── e2e-infra
          ├── test-race
          ├── helm-validate
          └── docs
```

## Data Flow: Change Detection

```
1. dorny/paths-filter evaluates e2e_chaos filter against PR diff
2. ci-resolve-changes.sh reads FILTER_e2e_chaos env var
3. Script emits e2e-chaos=true when:
   - FILTER_e2e_chaos=true (chaos test files changed), OR
   - go_changed=true (operator/shared code changed OR tag push)
4. e2e-chaos job evaluates: needs.changes.outputs.e2e-chaos == 'true'
```

## Pitfalls

- **GitHub Actions skipped-job behavior**: When `e2e-operator` is skipped (no operator changes), its `result` is `skipped` (not `failure`). The `!contains(needs.*.result, 'failure')` check correctly allows the chaos job to proceed. Do NOT use `success()` or `needs.e2e-operator.result == 'success'` as the condition — this would block chaos tests when e2e-operator is skipped.

- **Chaos Mesh already in deploy-infra**: The `deploy-infra.sh` script deploys Chaos Mesh as a HelmRelease. No additional Chaos Mesh installation step is needed. The `setup-e2e-infra` composite action handles everything.

- **Serial execution timing**: With `parallel: 1` in the chaos config and 5m assert timeouts, worst-case test execution is ~30 min (3 scenarios × ~10 min each). The 60-min job timeout provides adequate buffer for cluster setup.

## Key Files

- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — Main CI workflow (lines 39-109 for change detection, lines 336-411 for e2e-operator pattern)
- `hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh` — Change detection script (emit e2e-chaos output after line 73)
- `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` — Composite action reused by e2e-chaos job
- `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` — Operator deployment reused by e2e-chaos job
- `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` — Diagnostic script reused by e2e-chaos job
- `hack/ci-build-service-image.sh` — Service image build reused by e2e-chaos job
- `tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml` — Chaos-specific Chainsaw config (serial, extended timeouts)
- `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` — CI docs to update
- `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` — Chaos test docs to update
